claim: Video and image diffusion models don't require 50 denoising steps; distillation can reduce this to 4, 8, or even 1 step by training a student model to match teacher output, enabling 10-200x speedup.
actionable: Investigate step distillation for diffusion models if latency is critical; trade quality for speed by training a student model on fewer steps.
source_title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps

claim: Dynamic quantization (computing ranges on-the-fly) is preferred over static quantization for diffusion models to maintain image/video quality while reducing memory and improving performance.
actionable: When quantizing diffusion models, use dynamic quantization on NVIDIA's TensorRT LLM visual gen repository; pre-quantized checkpoints are available on Hugging Face.
source_title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps

claim: Chunk-based caching skips recomputing latent regions that change minimally between denoising steps, improving performance without sacrificing output quality if thresholds are tuned correctly.
actionable: Implement chunk-based caching in TensorRT LLM or VLLM for diffusion; set a threshold for change detection and experiment to maintain quality.
source_title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps

claim: Distribution-based distillation (allowing student to follow any path to match teacher's output) is now more common and achieves higher quality than trajectory-based distillation.
actionable: When distilling diffusion models, use distribution-based approaches; they're more flexible and produce better results than forcing exact trajectory matching.
source_title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps

claim: NVIDIA's FastGen toolkit packages distillation, quantization, and sharding for large video models (20-40B parameters); using it can reduce optimization complexity.
actionable: For large diffusion models, use FastGen for distillation and inference optimization; it handles multi-GPU sharding and data pipeline orchestration.
source_title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps

claim: Real-time video/image generation is achievable on a single Blackwell B200 GPU using combined quantization, caching, and distillation techniques.
actionable: If targeting real-time generation, stack quantization + caching + distillation; don't rely on any single technique alone.
source_title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps

claim: Optimization techniques (quantization, caching, distillation) are incremental and stackable; start with quantization, add caching if needed, then distillation if latency is still critical.
actionable: Approach diffusion optimization incrementally: test quantization alone, then add caching, then distillation; measure quality at each step to avoid over-optimization.
source_title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps
